title: Printing My Automation List
excerpt: I have almost 50 custom automations on a new board. Short of doing Print Screen a gazillion times, is there a way to print my list of automations. I’m just wanting to make sure I’ve created all the automations I need. Wh…

posts.author: SpreadsheetNinja (Julie Roundy)
posts.cookedHtml: I have almost 50 custom automations on a new board. Short of doing Print Screen a gazillion times, is there a way to print my list of automations. I’m just wanting to make sure I’ve created all the automations I need. When one is altered or duplicated/new, it hits at the top of the list so my like-kind automations aren’t grouped together anymore.

posts.author: Bice (Bice)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

while this may not be a direct answer…

I built a design system prototyping tool for Monday.com called Tuesday. You can import and export JSON and other data structures.

Maybe it can help you.

https://trytuesdaynow.com

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi I am also looking for this - is there a way to see this? I’ve checked admin console and didn’t find it.

posts.author: amesthename (Amy)
posts.cookedHtml: A way to see all of the automations a person owns would be very helpful, especially in the off boarding process. It asks me to switch automation ownership, but I have no way of knowing what automations I am taking over. I was expecting to find a report of all the automations I own somewhere in the Admin console, but I can only see integrations in the account.
